artifacts.summary: Bidirectional reachable fragment with full totality proof (832 lines, sorry-free)

partial_progress.details: Phases A-D completed. Phase E blocked on fundamental mathematical obstacle: chain-based approaches fail due to F-persistence problem. See handoff-v2 for detailed analysis.

blocker.description: Chain-based Int embedding fails because F-formulas don't persist through GContent-based chain steps. Lindenbaum extensions can kill F-formulas by introducing G(neg(phi)).
viable_options: Option A: Controlled Lindenbaum extension (requires proving enlarged seed consistency)
Option B: Maximize over chains using Zorn's lemma
Option C: Semantic approach bypassing BFMCS Int
Option D: Generalize satisfiable to Preorder D (architecture change)

completion_data.completion_summary: Phases A-D completed (fragment FMCS sorry-free). Phase E blocked on mathematical obstacle: Int embedding requires chain closed under F/P witnesses, but F-persistence fails in all chain approaches.

roadmap_items.title: Resolve F-persistence mathematical obstacle
roadmap_items.description: Choose and implement viable option from handoff-v2: controlled Lindenbaum, Zorn on chains, semantic bypass, or architecture change to Preorder D

review_reason: Fundamental mathematical obstacle identified. The standard chain-based approach for Int embedding is mathematically impossible due to F-formula non-persistence. User needs to decide between architectural options.
